content.problemStatement:
It is the final day of La Liga. Chef has a certain criteria for assessing football matches.

In particular, he only likes a match if:


The match ends in a draw, and,
**At least** one goal has been scored by either team.
Given the goals scored by both the teams as XXX and YYY respectively, determine whether Chef will like the match or not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the goals scored by each team.


Output Format
For each test case, output YES\texttt{YES}YES if Chef will like the match, else output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It is a draw in which both teams have scored a goal, Chef will like this match.
Test case 222: The game is not a draw. Hence, Chef will not like this match.
Test case 333: Neither team scored a goal, so Chef will not like this match.
Test case 444: It is a draw in which both teams scored 222 goals each. Chef will like this match.
